hypothesis: If battery property predictors use composition-weighted molecular embeddings from large-scale SMILES-pretrained chemical foundation models, then they will achieve higher accuracy, stronger out-of-distribution performance, and more faithful moiety–property correlations across molecular, formulation, interfacial, and device-level tasks than Morgan fingerprints or smaller graph/text baselines, because large-scale pretraining encodes transferable chemical knowledge that generalizes across scales.
